# contentType

**Instance Property**

The content type of the source data for the thumbnail request.

## Declaration

```swift
var contentType: UTType! { get set }
```

## Discussion

Quick Look Thumbnailing uses the content type of the source data to determine the provider of the thumbnail and the icon styles it applies when you request [doc://com.apple.quicklookthumbnailing/documentation/QuickLookThumbnailing/QLThumbnailGenerator/Request/iconMode].

If you don’t set this property, Quick Look Thumbnailing derives the content from the file extension. When the file doesn’t have a meaningful extension and you know the content type, set the property directly.

## Describing the Requested Thumbnail

- **size**: The size of the thumbnails.
- **scale**: The pixel density of the display on the intended device.
- **representationTypes**: The thumbnail sizes that you provide for a thumbnail request.
- **minimumDimension**: The minimum height or width for a generated thumbnail.
- **iconMode**: A Boolean value indicating whether the generated thumbnail request should include icon decorations.
- **QLThumbnailGenerator.Request.RepresentationTypes**: The various types of thumbnails that you can request.
